title: Yale - Blood Urea Nitrogen
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"39ffg","text":"Summary: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1fjdh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3smmc","text":"Procedure used to measure the concentration of Blood Urea Nitrogen(BUN) in blood, plasma, and serum. Urea is determined by the enzymatically coupled reactions of urease (to form ammonia) and glutamate dehydrogenase (conversion of ammonia and glutamate to glutamine with oxidation of NADH to NAD). The rate of NAD formation is monitored by the change in absorbance at 340 nm. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Calibrate Cobas for BUN analysis by running a multi analyte standard and two assayed control serums.

Sample handling as performed by the Cobas Mira Plus. a) Cobas pipettes 2 µL of sample into a cuvette slot. b) Absorbance is measured at 340 nm. c) Add 200 µL of BUN liquid reagent. d) Mixture is incubated at 37ºC for 10 minutes. e) Absorbance is measured at 340 nm. Change in absorbance is calculated.
creator: Lili Liang
